Grape___Black_rot.description: A serious fungal disease that attacks all green parts of the vine.
Grape___Black_rot.symptoms:
• Small brown spots on leaves
• Fruit turns black and shrivels into mummies
• Circular lesions on shoots
Grape___Black_rot.prevention:
• Remove mummified berries
• Prune for better sunlight
• Keep area around vines clean
Grape___Black_rot.treatment: Apply fungicides like Myclobutanil or Mancozeb before bloom.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).description: A complex disease affecting the wood and leaves, often causing tiger-stripe patterns.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms:
• Interveinal yellowing (tiger stripes)
• Small dark spots on fruit skin
• Sudden wilting (apoplexy)
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention:
• Disinfect pruning tools
• Avoid pruning during wet weather
• Remove dead vines
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).treatment: No direct cure; focus on trunk surgery or vine replacement.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.description: Fungal infection causing irregular brown spots on leaves, leading to premature leaf fall.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms:
• Small, irregular reddish-brown spots
• Spots merge to form large dead areas
• Leaves drop prematurely
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention:
• Destroy infected leaves
• Use resistant varieties
• Avoid dense foliage

Maize___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_s.description: One of the most significant yield-limiting diseases of corn worldwide.
Maize___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_s.symptoms:
• Rectangular tan spots
• Spots parallel to leaf veins
• Grayish cast to lesions
Maize___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_s.prevention:
• Crop rotation
• Tillage to bury residue
• Hybrid selection
Maize___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray_leaf_s.treatment: Apply foliar fungicides like Pyraclostrobin during silking.

Rice___Bacterial_Leaf_Blight.description: A highly destructive bacterial disease favored by high humidity and rain.
Rice___Bacterial_Leaf_Blight.symptoms:
• Yellow-orange stripes on leaf edges
• Wavy margins on lesions
• Milky bacterial ooze in morning
Rice___Bacterial_Leaf_Blight.prevention:
• Balanced nitrogen use
• Seed treatment with bleaching powder
• Proper drainage
Rice___Bacterial_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Spray Streptocycline (6g) mixed with Copper Oxychloride (500g) per acre.

Rice___Bakanae_Disease.description: A seed-borne fungal disease causing plants to grow abnormally tall and thin.
Rice___Bakanae_Disease.symptoms:
• Tall, spindly, pale yellow plants
• Poor root development
• Seedling death
Rice___Bakanae_Disease.prevention:
• Use certified disease-free seeds
• Seed treatment with fungicides
• Remove infected plants
Rice___Bakanae_Disease.treatment: Soak seeds in Carbendazim solution before sowing.

Rice___Blast.description: One of the most devastating rice diseases globally, affecting all growth stages.
Rice___Blast.symptoms:
• Diamond-shaped spots with gray centers
• Neck rot (breaking of panicles)
• Brown lesions on nodes
Rice___Blast.prevention:
• Avoid excessive Nitrogen
• Burn infected crop residue
• Use resistant varieties
Rice___Blast.treatment: Spray Tricyclazole 75 WP at 0.6g/liter of water.

Rice___Brown_Plant_Hopper.description: A pest that sucks sap, causing 'hopper burn' and spreading viruses.
Rice___Brown_Plant_Hopper.symptoms:
• Circular patches of drying plants
• Plants turn yellow then brown
• Visible insects at the base of stalks
Rice___Brown_Plant_Hopper.prevention:
• Alternate wetting and drying of fields
• Avoid dense planting
• Conserve natural enemies (spiders)
Rice___Brown_Plant_Hopper.treatment: Apply Pymetrozine or Dinotefuran at the base of the plants.

Rice___Brown_Spot.description: Fungal disease often associated with nutrient-deficient soil.
Rice___Brown_Spot.symptoms:
• Small oval brown spots
• Spots look like sesame seeds
• Reduced grain quality
Rice___Brown_Spot.prevention:
• Apply balanced fertilizers (NKP)
• Seed treatment with Carbendazim
• Improve soil health
Rice___Brown_Spot.treatment: Spray Mancozeb or Edifenphos if symptoms are widespread.

Rice___False_Smut.description: Fungal disease that transforms individual grains into orange/black velvety balls.
Rice___False_Smut.symptoms:
• Large greenish-yellow balls on grains
• Balls turn orange then dark green/black
• Only a few grains per panicle affected
Rice___False_Smut.prevention:
• Use clean seeds
• Early planting
• Moderate nitrogen use
Rice___False_Smut.treatment: Spray Copper Oxychloride or Propiconazole at booting stage.

Rice___Nitrogen_Deficiency.description: Nutrient deficiency causing overall yellowing and poor growth.
Rice___Nitrogen_Deficiency.symptoms:
• Older leaves turn light green to yellow
• Stunted growth
• Thin stems and few tillers
Rice___Nitrogen_Deficiency.prevention:
• Soil testing
• Split application of Urea
• Use of Green Manures
Rice___Nitrogen_Deficiency.treatment: Top-dress with Urea or apply Neem-coated Urea.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.hindi_name: टमाटर का पीला पत्ता मरोड़ वायरस
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.description: A viral disease spread by whiteflies that can cause 100% crop loss.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms:
• Leaves curl upward and inward
• Intense yellowing of leaf edges
• Stunted plant growth
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention:
• Use whitefly-proof mesh
• Yellow sticky traps
• Remove silverleaf nightshade weeds
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: Control whiteflies using Neem oil or systemic insecticides.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.description: Fungal disease common in high humidity and poor ventilation.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms:
• Pale green spots on upper leaf surface
• Olive-green velvety mold on underside
• Leaves wither and drop
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention:
• Improve greenhouse ventilation
• Avoid wetting leaves
• Resistant varieties
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Spray fungicides like Chlorothalonil or Difenoconazole.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: Viral disease that causes mottled patterns on leaves and reduces fruit quality.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms:
• Mottled light and dark green patterns
• Fern-like leaf growth
• Internal browning of fruit
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention:
• Do not smoke near plants
• Wash hands before handling
• Use certified virus-free seeds
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: No cure for virus; remove and burn infected plants immediately.

not_a_leaf.description: The AI model did not detect a plant leaf in the photo.
not_a_leaf.symptoms:
• Blurred object
• Background noise
• Non-plant object
not_a_leaf.treatment: Please retake the photo focusing clearly on a single diseased leaf.

healthy.description: The crop appears healthy with no visible signs of disease.
healthy.symptoms:
• Vibrant green leaves
• Strong stems
• Regular growth pattern
healthy.prevention:
• Regular monitoring
• Balanced fertilization
• Proper watering
healthy.treatment: Continue regular maintenance and preventive care.
